question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /construction/submit endpoint with concurrent requests such that the NodeMap-backed SDK client's single-attempt (SetMaxAttempts(1)) exhausts all available goroutines, causing legitimate transactions to never be gossiped to any node in the NodeMap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.Nodes (NodeMap)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted signed transaction whose embedded node account ID does not match any entry in NodeMap, causing the SDK to silently drop the gossip attempt with no retry since SetMaxAttempts(1) is set, resulting in the transaction never reaching consensus?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default 24h NodeRefreshInterval by submitting transactions during a window when all nodes in the cached NodeMap have rotated their endpoints, causing every gossip attempt to fail for up to 24 hours until the address book refreshes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.Online] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where the Online flag is evaluated as false at transaction submission time (e.g., via a race condition during startup), causing ConstructionSubmit to return ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode and silently prevent gossip of the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Response{}.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user craft a block query that causes the block service to truncate transactions beyond MaxTransactionsInBlock into OtherTransactions, effectively hiding those transactions from block consumers and preventing downstream systems from detecting that gossip occurred?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Http{}.ReadTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send a slow-loris style HTTP request that holds the ReadTimeout window open, consuming all available HTTP server connections and preventing legitimate /construction/submit requests from being processed, thus blocking gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Http{}.WriteTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the WriteTimeout expires before the SDK gossip response is written back to the caller, causing the transaction submission to appear failed on the client side even though the gossip may or may not have occurred, leading to double-submit or dropped transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Http{}.IdleTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of idle keep-alive connections up to the IdleTimeout limit, exhausting the server's connection pool and preventing new /construction/submit requests from being accepted, thereby blocking gossip of all pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Pool{}.MaxOpenConnections] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the database Pool.MaxOpenConnections by sending many concurrent read-heavy requests (e.g., /block or /account/balance), starving the construction service of DB connections needed to validate accounts before gossiping transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Pool{}.MaxIdleConnections] Can an unprivileged external user cause Pool.MaxIdleConnections to be fully occupied by long-running idle queries, forcing new transaction-submission DB lookups to wait indefinitely, delaying or preventing gossip of time-sensitive transactions whose validity window expires?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Db{}.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /block or /account request that triggers a slow DB query exceeding StatementTimeout, causing the DB connection to be killed and leaving the construction service in a degraded state where subsequent gossip-related DB lookups also fail?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDsn() always sets sslmode=disable to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the DB connection, injecting false account state that causes the construction service to reject valid transactions before they are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node account ID that maps to a non-responsive or blackholed IP in the NodeMap, causing the single gossip attempt (SetMaxAttempts(1)) to time out and the transaction to be permanently dropped without any retry?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a graceful shutdown (e.g., by sending a signal via a side-channel or exploiting a crash) during which in-flight gossip attempts are abandoned because ShutdownTimeout is too short, causing multiple transactions to never reach consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Cache{}.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user flood the entity cache (EntityCacheKey) with requests for non-existent account IDs, evicting legitimate cached entries and forcing expensive DB lookups on every transaction submission, creating enough latency that transaction validity windows expire before gossip occurs?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 24h default NodeRefreshInterval to submit transactions immediately after a known network topology change, when the NodeMap still contains stale node endpoints, causing all gossip attempts to fail for the entire refresh interval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Response{}.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user set MaxTransactionsInBlock to 0 via an environment variable (HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK=0) if the server reads env vars without validation, causing all transactions in every block to be moved to OtherTransactions and never returned in the primary block response, preventing gossip verification?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.Network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Network field is a plain string with no enum validation at the type level, submitting a crafted network identifier that causes ClientForName to fail silently, resulting in an SDK client that cannot gossip any transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node account ID of 0.0.0 (zero value of hiero.AccountID), which may not be present in NodeMap, causing the gossip to be directed to a non-existent node and the transaction to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Http{}.ReadHeaderTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send HTTP requests with extremely large or fragmented headers that consume the ReadHeaderTimeout window, causing the server to close connections before the transaction body is read, preventing gossip of the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.Nodes (NodeMap)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NodeMap is a map (unordered) and the SDK picks a random node, by timing transaction submissions to coincide with periods when the randomly selected node is known to be unreachable, causing gossip failures with no retry?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Pool{}.MaxLifetime] Can an unprivileged external user trigger rapid connection churn by submitting many short requests that cause DB connections to hit MaxLifetime and be recycled, creating a window where no connections are available for the construction service to validate and gossip a transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.Online] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the Online flag being set and the SDK client being fully initialized, submitting a transaction during this window that bypasses the online check but uses an uninitialized SDK client, causing gossip to fail silently?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a batch of transactions all targeting the same single node in NodeMap, overwhelming that node's gRPC endpoint and causing it to reject subsequent gossip requests, effectively preventing those transactions from reaching consensus?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Config{}.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeRefreshInterval by repeatedly triggering address book refresh failures (e.g., by exhausting DB connections), causing the NodeMap to remain empty and all subsequent gossip attempts to fail?
